name: Buddy Johnson
description: American jazz and New York blues pianist and bandleader (1915–1977)
abstract: Woodrow Wilson "Buddy" Johnson (January 10, 1915 – February 9, 1977) was an American jump blues pianist and bandleader active from the 1930s through the 1960s. His songs were often performed by his sister Ella Johnson, most notably "Since I Fell for You", which became a jazz standard, as did "Save Your Love For Me".

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert

works_created.t: Ever Since the One I Love's Been Gone

about_quotes.speaker: Etta Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I got the job... Buddy Johnson played the piano... I was with Buddy Johnson’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Etta Jones interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: When I wrote for the band, he let me write anything I wanted to write... Then he asked me to come to New York with him. I finally went with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alfred Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him on the Roof Garden, Maurice Durand and I, and Buddy Johnson... We worked on the Roof Garden two years from 25 to 27.

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: I played with that band until Buddy Johnson heard me. And then he came and took me to New York City.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Leonard Bocage
about_quotes.evidence: And they had, uh, Peter on trumpet... we had a fellow named Buddy Johnson and Harrison Bones.

about_quotes.speaker: Maurice Durand
about_quotes.evidence: We had, uh, Bebe Mathis on drum. Willie, uh, Buddy Johnson, trombone, I'm playing trumpet.
